name: Happy Cat Public Storefront Host

securitySheaf.label: Probe IC
securitySheaf.effect: Host starts checking the intruder icon and access path.

securitySheaf.label: Trace IC
securitySheaf.effect: Host attempts to establish or accelerate a trace.

securitySheaf.label: Scramble IC
securitySheaf.effect: Host interferes with files, memory, or the current operation.

securitySheaf.label: Store-brand Tar Baby
securitySheaf.effect: Nuisance IC attempts to hold the icon in place for follow-up.

sculpting: Bright retail storefront, mascot popups, collectible-card catalog, snack ads, and local teen chatter overlays.
notes: First live lead from Session 2026-07-09. Small-business host near dense public Matrix traffic.

nodes.title: Local Grid Outside Happy Cat
nodes.description: You hover in the east Nashville grid among cheap public icons, snack ads, apartment-block chatter, and the glowing storefront sign for Happy Cat.

choices.label: Open the Happy Cat public door

nodes.title: Mascot Storefront
nodes.description: A lucky-cat mascot bows from behind a neon counter. Card packs orbit like menu icons; snack ads crawl along the floor like animated receipt tape.

choices.label: Search customer chatter and teen hangout traces
Back out to the local grid

nodes.title: Sticker-Covered Traffic Cloud
nodes.description: Speech bubbles, sticker tags, purchase pings, and public-grid noise float around the storefront. Most of it is teenage pose and retail spam, but patterns may be hiding in the clutter.

nodes.title: Customer Chatter Boards
nodes.description: Message-board cards flip past: collectible trades, snack complaints, local gossip, and repeated absences where vandal tags should have been.

nodes.title: Locked Stockroom Door
nodes.description: Behind the cute storefront is a locked staff-only door: camera logs, shift notes, vendor orders, and back-office records stacked like sealed card boxes.

nodes.title: Back Office Cabinet
nodes.description: Receipts, employee notes, camera thumbnails, and supplier records sit in neat rows. The useful question is not what was hit, but why this place was not.

nodes.title: Register and Camera Hooks
nodes.description: The register, door chime, camera feed, and display shelves hang as simple device icons. They are mundane, but mundane things remember who walked past.

nodes.title: Neon Static Where Tags Should Be
nodes.description: The absence has a shape: a clean patch in the vandalism wave, too neat to be pure luck. Either someone avoided Happy Cat, protected it, or used it as a marker.

choices.label: Follow the static toward possible Pixel Sticks traces

nodes.title: Useful Pattern Cache
nodes.description: You have enough strands to hand the GM a concrete lead: traffic, receipts, cameras, or missing-tag geometry pointing to the next branch of the run.

choices.label: Keep digging and risk the sheaf

nodes.title: Clean Exit
nodes.description: The storefront folds back into neon signs and public chatter. If the tally stayed low, nobody knows the mascot let you look behind the counter.
